channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Tejas Mk1A Set to Get X-Guard Towed Decoy as IAF Adapts to Home-On-Jam Missile Threat
item.description: The Indian Air Force is moving to significantly upgrade the survivability of its Tejas fleet by evaluating the integration of the Israeli X-Guard towed decoy system on the Tejas Mk1A, reflecting hard lessons drawn from recent aerial engagements. The shift comes after internal electronic warfare suites and traditional onboard jammers were found to be increasingly vulnerable against modern “home-on-jam” (HOJ) missile seekers, which are designed to lock onto the very jamming signals meant to protect the aircraft.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russia Offers 1,500 km Kalibr-PL Cruise Missile to Indian Navy for Submarine Deep-Strike Capability
item.description: idrw.org has learned from Russian industry sources that Moscow has formally pitched the 3M-14E Kalibr-PL submarine-launched land-attack cruise missile to the Indian Navy in response to its 2026 Request for Information (RFI) for long-range Submarine-Launched Land-Attack Missiles (LACM). The offer is being positioned as a high-capability, ready-to-induct solution that significantly exceeds the Navy’s baseline requirements and could rapidly enhance India’s underwater deep-strike arsenal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dynamatic Technologies Unveils Plans for ‘Super Kaatil’ Supersonic Loitering Munition with 350 km Range
item.description: Dynamatic Technologies Ltd has disclosed plans to develop a next-generation loitering munition dubbed “Super Kaatil,” signaling a major leap in India’s unmanned strike capabilities. Positioned as a supersonic unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), the system is designed to combine high-speed penetration with autonomous strike precision, significantly expanding the operational envelope of loitering munitions in contested environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: How Israeli, Indian and American Precision-Guided Weapons Are Turning Indian Navy’s MiG-29K Fleet into a Carrier-Killer Force
item.description: The Indian Navy’s carrier-borne MiG-29K fleet, though limited to roughly 40 aircraft, is steadily evolving into a highly capable, multi-role strike force through the integration of Israeli, Indian, and American precision-guided munitions. This layered weapons ecosystem is transforming the aircraft from a traditional naval fighter into a networked, precision-strike platform capable of holding high-value targets—including hostile aircraft carriers—at risk.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Beyond Mogami: Japan’s Export Reforms Bring UNICORN Masts and Sensors into Focus for India
item.description: Japan’s sweeping relaxation of its decades-old arms export restrictions, approved by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s cabinet on 21 April 2026, marks the most significant shift in Tokyo’s defence policy since the end of World War II. By scrapping the rigid “five-category” framework that confined exports largely to non-lethal equipment such as rescue, transport, surveillance and minesweeping systems, Japan can now sell complete warships, missiles, fighter jets and other lethal platforms to trusted partners—including India—subject only to case-by-case government approval and end-use controls.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pinaka Mk-III vs EXTRA: DRDO Says India’s 120 km Rocket is 60% Cheaper then Israeli Rocket
item.description: In a significant boost to India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative in artillery, the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has confirmed that the upcoming 120 km range Guided Pinaka Mk-III (Long Range Guided Rocket – LRGR) will be substantially more economical than the Israeli-origin EXTRA rockets procured as the interim Suryastra system. This cost advantage positions the indigenous Pinaka family as the long-term backbone for the Indian Army’s precision deep-strike capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Aridhaman Commissioning Puts India in Elite SSBN Club Amid Commercial Shipbuilding Challenges
item.description: India has taken a giant leap in maritime power with the recent commissioning of INS Aridhaman, its third indigenously built nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine, marking a proud entry deeper into the exclusive club of nations capable of constructing and operating such strategic underwater platforms. This milestone strengthens the country’s nuclear triad and sea-based deterrence capabilities at a time when regional security dynamics demand robust naval presence.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russian oil import data ‘confidential’, exempt under RTI, says CIC
item.description: Data on oil imports from Russia to India cannot be provided as it is exempted under the RTI Act due to its “commercial and confidential” nature, according to the Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC) under the Oil Ministry, with the Central Information Commission too backing it, citing strategic and economic interests of the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s position on Palestine unchanged; no consensus on West Asia at Brics
item.description: Sharp differences among the BRICS member states over the US-Israel war on Iran stalled India’s efforts to build a consensus position on the conflict during a meeting of the grouping in New Delhi last week, official sources said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Private Sector Space Investment Crosses $600 Million In 5 Years: Dr. Jitendra Singh
item.description: India’s space sector has witnessed over $600 million in private investment in the last five years, Union Minister of State Dr. Jitendra Singh said on Sunday while reviewing initiatives to strengthen the country’s space ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NSA Ajit Doval discusses regional situation with UAE Prez Zayed Al Nahyan
item.description: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval on Sunday met UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and discussed measures to deepen the comprehensive strategic partnership and the regional situation. The Indian Embassy in the UAE said Doval conveyed Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s greetings to the UAE leader during his official visit to the Arab country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India provides watercraft to Sri Lankan Coast Guard for search and rescue
item.description: India handed over two Personal Watercrafts (PWCs) and safety gear to the Sri Lanka Coast Guard on Sunday. The Indian government is assisting in enhancing the Sri Lanka Coast Guard’s capability in near-shore Search and Rescue (SAR), the High Commission of India in Colombo said on its X handle.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Naval Ship arrives in Singapore under IOS SAGAR mission
item.description: Indian Naval Ship Sunayna arrived at Singapore’s Changi Naval Base for a port call as part of the IOS SAGAR initiative, said the High Commission of India in Singapore on Sunday. The High Commissioner visited IOS SAGAR and interacted with members from partner navies of the Indian Ocean Region onboard IOS SAGAR.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran Airspace Partially Open, More Flights Coming To India From Gulf
item.description: Iran’s airspace is partially open for cargo and chartered flights, but Indian nationals are advised to avoid travelling to the country, and those already there are urged to leave via the land border, with the embassy’s support, the Ministry of External Affairs said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Arunachal CM Khandu meets Rajnath Singh, seeks boost to border infra
item.description: Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu on Sunday met Defence Minister Rajnath Singh in New Delhi, urging accelerated development of border infrastructure in the state. The Defence Minister assured that the proposed projects would be examined and taken up on priority.
item.dc:creator: admin
